UK Biobank health data keeps ending up on GitHub
items.summary:
I'm a researcher studying privacy, and I started tracking the DMCA notices that UK Biobank sends to GitHub. I tracked 110 notices filed so far, targeting 197 code repositories by 170 developers across the world.

The exposure of Biobank data on GitHub is the latest in a long series of governan

top_events.narrative_summary: Gold has surged to a new all-time high of $4,800/oz, driven by a weakening U.S. dollar and subdued oil prices below $100. The move is confirmed cross-source (Western futures + Chinese spot), with Chinese gold miners already guiding higher 2024 production in anticipation. This breakout on macro tailwinds (dollar weakness, disinflation signal from cheap oil) suggests a structural bid, not just a speculative spike.

Iran geopolitical tensions are creating cross-asset volatility, with Trump warning Iran to reach a deal while markets await a formal address on potential military escalation. Gold is caught between safe-haven demand (softer USD) and risk-off selling in mining equities, signaling markets are pricing in uncertainty rather than a definitive outcome. This is a classic geopolitical headline risk event where the actual policy action will determine direction.

Apple officially announced CEO succession, with John Ternus (SVP of Hardware Engineering) set to take over from Tim Cook in September 2026, while Cook transitions to Executive Chairman. The move signals a hardware-first strategic pivot amid Apple's AI catch-up challenges and intensifying competition from Huawei in China, with Cook's continued chairmanship providing transition stability.

Strait of Hormuz geopolitical tensions are driving dual safe-haven flows — gold targeting $4,800 on USD strength and supply-disruption fears, while Bitcoin gains legitimacy as an alternative payment rail for tanker tolls, signaling crypto adoption in commodity trade infrastructure. This convergence of traditional and crypto safe havens around a single chokepoint event suggests markets are pricing in sustained disruption risk to global energy flows.

Multiple Chinese AI labs are simultaneously releasing competitive frontier models — MiniMax M2.7 (self-improving training loops), Zhipu GLM 5.1, and Xiaomi MiMo (open to developers via agent frameworks). This signals an accelerating domestic AI arms race in China, compressing the capability gap with US labs and expanding the addressable market for Chinese AI infrastructure and semiconductor demand.
